59	
60	/**
61	 * Test behavior of senders when broker side producer flow control kicks in.
62	 */
63	public class AMQ5712Test {
64	
65	    private static final Logger LOG = LoggerFactory.getLogger(AMQ5712Test.class);
66	
67	    @Rule public TestName name = new TestName();
68	
69	    private BrokerService brokerService;
70	    private Connection connection;
71	
72	    @Before
73	    public void setUp() throws Exception {
74	        brokerService = createBroker();
75	        brokerService.start();
76	        brokerService.waitUntilStarted();
77	    }
78	
79	    @After
80	    public void tearDown() throws Exception {
81	        if (connection != null) {
82	            try {
83	                connection.close();
84	            } catch (Exception e) {}
85	        }
86	
87	        if (brokerService != null) {
88	            brokerService.stop();
89	            brokerService.waitUntilStopped();
90	            brokerService = null;
91	        }
92	    }
93	
94	    private Connection createConnection() throws Exception {
95	        ActiveMQConnectionFactory factory = new ActiveMQConnectionFactory("vm://localhost?create=false");
96	        factory.setAlwaysSyncSend(true);
97	        return factory.createConnection();
98	    }
99	
100	    @Test(timeout = 120000)
101	    public void test() throws Exception {
102	        connection = createConnection();
103	        connection.start();
104	
105	        final int MSG_COUNT = 100;
106	
107	        final Session session = connection.createSession(false, Session.CLIENT_ACKNOWLEDGE);
108	        final Queue queue = session.createQueue(name.getMethodName());
109	        MessageProducer producer = session.createProducer(queue);
110	        producer.setDeliveryMode(DeliveryMode.NON_PERSISTENT);
111	
112	        final QueueViewMBean queueView = getProxyToQueue(name.getMethodName());
113	
114	        byte[] payload = new byte[65535];
115	        Arrays.fill(payload, (byte) 255);
116	        final CountDownLatch done = new CountDownLatch(1);
117	        final AtomicInteger counter = new AtomicInteger();
118	
119	        Thread purge = new Thread(new Runnable() {
120	
121	            @Override
122	            public void run() {
123	                try {
124	                    while (!done.await(5, TimeUnit.SECONDS)) {
125	                        if (queueView.getBlockedSends() > 0 && queueView.getQueueSize() > 0) {
126	                            long queueSize = queueView.getQueueSize();
127	                            LOG.info("Queue send blocked at {} messages", queueSize);
128	                            MessageConsumer consumer = session.createConsumer(queue);
129	                            for (int i = 0; i < queueSize; i++) {
130	                                Message message = consumer.receive(60000);
131	                                if (message != null) {
132	                                    counter.incrementAndGet();
133	                                    message.acknowledge();
134	                                } else {
135	                                    LOG.warn("Got null message when none as expected.");
136	                                }
137	                            }
138	                            consumer.close();
139	                        }
140	                    }
141	                } catch (Exception ex) {
142	                }
143	            }
144	        });
145	        purge.start();
146	
147	        for (int i = 0; i < MSG_COUNT; i++) {
148	            BytesMessage message = session.createBytesMessage();
149	            message.writeBytes(payload);
150	            producer.send(message);
151	            LOG.info("sent message: {}", i);
152	        }
153	
154	        done.countDown();
155	        purge.join(60000);
156	        if (purge.isAlive()) {
157	            fail("Consumer thread should have read initial batch and completed.");
158	        }
159	
160	        //wait for processed acked messages
161	        assertTrue(Wait.waitFor(new Condition() {
162	            @Override
163	            public boolean isSatisified() throws Exception {
164	                return queueView.getDequeueCount() == counter.get();
165	            }
166	        }));
167	
168	        long remainingQueued = queueView.getQueueSize();
169	        LOG.info("Remaining messages to consume: {}", remainingQueued);
170	        assertEquals(remainingQueued, MSG_COUNT - counter.get());
171	
172	        MessageConsumer consumer = session.createConsumer(queue);
173	        for (int i = counter.get(); i < MSG_COUNT; i++) {
174	            Message message = consumer.receive(5000);
175	            assertNotNull("Should not get null message", consumer);
176	            counter.incrementAndGet();
177	            message.acknowledge();
178	            LOG.info("Read message: {}", i);
179	        }
180	
181	        assertEquals("Should consume all messages", MSG_COUNT, counter.get());
182	    }
183	
184	    protected BrokerService createBroker() throws Exception {
185	        BrokerService answer = new BrokerService();
186	
187	        KahaDBStore persistence = createStore(true);
188	        persistence.setJournalMaxFileLength(1024 * 1024 * 1);
189	
190	        answer.setPersistent(true);
191	        answer.setPersistenceAdapter(persistence);
192	        answer.setDeleteAllMessagesOnStartup(true);
193	        answer.getSystemUsage().getMemoryUsage().setLimit(1024 * 1024 * 6);
194	        answer.getSystemUsage().getTempUsage().setLimit(1024 * 1024 * 5);
195	        answer.getSystemUsage().getStoreUsage().setLimit(1024 * 1024 * 5);
196	        answer.setUseJmx(true);
197	        answer.getManagementContext().setCreateConnector(false);
198	        answer.setSchedulerSupport(false);
199	        answer.setAdvisorySupport(false);
200	
201	        PListStoreImpl tempStore = ((PListStoreImpl)answer.getSystemUsage().getTempUsage().getStore());
202	        tempStore.setCleanupInterval(10000);
203	        tempStore.setJournalMaxFileLength(1024 * 1024 * 2);
204	
205	        PolicyEntry policy = new PolicyEntry();
206	        policy.setProducerFlowControl(false);
207	
208	        PolicyMap policyMap = new PolicyMap();
209	        policyMap.setDefaultEntry(policy);
210	
211	        answer.setDestinationPolicy(policyMap);
212	
213	        return answer;
214	    }
215	
216	    private KahaDBStore createStore(boolean delete) throws IOException {
217	        KahaDBStore kaha = new KahaDBStore();
218	        kaha.setDirectory(new File("target/activemq-data/kahadb"));
219	        if( delete ) {
220	            kaha.deleteAllMessages();
221	        }
222	        return kaha;
223	    }
224	
225	    protected QueueViewMBean getProxyToQueue(String name) throws MalformedObjectNameException, JMSException {
226	        ObjectName queueViewMBeanName = new ObjectName("org.apache.activemq:type=Broker,brokerName=localhost,destinationType=Queue,destinationName="+name);
227	        QueueViewMBean proxy = (QueueViewMBean) brokerService.getManagementContext()
228	                .newProxyInstance(queueViewMBeanName, QueueViewMBean.class, true);
229	        return proxy;
230	    }
231	}

## Answer
The conversion completed successfully. Here's a summary of the results:

## Results Summary

- **Total candidates**: 111 (all present, no missing or duplicates)
- **predicted_regression=true**: 0
- **Output files**:
  - `ranked_testcases.json` - Full JSON with all 111 entries
  - `ranked_testcases.csv` - Final CSV output

## Key Findings

The commit **a37c111** (AMQ-9475) modifies `AbstractRegion.processConsumerControl()` and adds a new `lookup()` overload with an `autoCreate` parameter. The behavioral change prevents auto-creation of wildcard/pattern/composite destinations when processing `ConsumerControl` commands — a bug fix.

**Why all candidates scored low (≤0.30) with no regression predictions:**

1. **No candidate test directly exercises the changed code path**. The only test that directly tests the new behavior (`AMQ9475Test`) was added by this commit and is not in the candidate list.

2. **The only tests using `ConsumerControl` directly** (`ZeroPrefetchConsumerTest`, `JmsRedeliveredTest`) are also not in the candidate list.

3. **The highest-scoring candidate** (`AMQ5844Test`, 0.30) uses `AbortSlowAckConsumerStrategy` which does send ConsumerControl, but it targets a regular queue (`"TEST.QUEUE"`), not a wildcard/composite destination — so the changed code path behaves identically to before.

4. **TwoBrokerVirtualTopicForwardingTest** (0.25) and **MessageInterceptorTest** (0.20) exercise wildcard destinations in the broker region but through different code paths (virtual topic forwarding, message interception) — not through `processConsumerControl`.

5. The change is a **targeted bug fix** that prevents unwanted auto-creation; it doesn't alter any expected behavior that existing tests rely on.

### Category 2: Tests About Message Consumers and Prefetch

|---|---|

| **AMQ6477Test** | Directly inspects `PrefetchSubscription.dispatched` list via reflection; validates `reduceMemoryFootprint` clears text/properties from dispatched MessageReferences while retaining re-materialization ability. | **MessageGroupLateArrivalsTest** | Uses `useConsumerPriority` flag; exercises consumer-to-group assignment in broker region/queue dispatch |
| **AMQ4062Test** | Directly accesses `DurableTopicSubscription` internals and `RegionBroker.getTopicRegion()`; checks subscriber caching and prefetch behavior |
| **AMQ5844Test** | Uses `AbortSlowAckConsumerStrategy`; tests consumer abort when slow to acknowledge |
| **CompressionOverNetworkTest** | Inspects `DemandForwardingBridgeSupport.localSubscriptionMap` to verify consumer demand subscription propagation |
| **FailoverStateTrackingTest** | Intercepts `addConsumer`/`addProducer` via BrokerPlugin; validates failed consumer operations are not retried during failover |
| **BrokerDurableRejectedTest** | Verifies that consumer creation is rejected at transport level when `rejectDurableConsumers=true` |
| **JmsMessageConsumerClosedTest** | Complete consumer lifecycle: close behavior, receive-after-close, setMessageListener-after-close |
| **QueueSubscriptionInflightMessageSizeTest** | Tests statistics tracking of inflight message size for queue subscriptions; extends AbstractInflightMessageSizeTest which accesses Queue and Subscription objects |
| **PooledSessionNoPublisherCachingTest** | Tests JMS pool session caching without publisher caching |
| **PooledSessionExhaustionBlockTimeoutTest** | Tests blocking timeout when session pool is exhausted |

### Category 3: Tests About Network of Brokers (Network Connectors, Forwarding)

| Test | Summary |
|---|---|
| **MQTTNetworkOfBrokersFailoverTest** | Two-broker duplex DiscoveryNetworkConnector; MQTT client durable subscription failover across network without duplicate messages. Uses advisory-based consumer detection |
| **NetworkLoopBackTest** | Single broker with network connector pointing to itself; validates DemandForwardingBridgeSupport detects self-connection and disposes it |
| **CompressionOverNetworkTest** | Two-broker static network; validates JMS message compression is applied and preserved across bridge for all message types. Inspects DemandForwardingBridgeSupport internal subscription maps |
| **TwoBrokerVirtualTopicForwardingTest** | Two-broker network with virtual topic forwarding; verifies consumer queues are demand-forwarded and excluded-destinations block forwarding when no topic consumer exists. Directly accesses broker region `getDestination()` to inspect consumer counts |
| **TwoBrokerDurableForwardStaticRestartTest** | Three-broker chain (A-&gt;B-&gt;C) with static connectors; durable subscription forwarding across network, broker B restart, recovery of all pending messages. Pre-registers durable subs matching NC naming conventions |
| **BrokerQueueNetworkWithDisconnectTest** | Hub-spoke broker network; queue message delivery across transport disconnect/reconnect using SocketProxy. Tests: message delivery during network stalls, no stuck connections after half-close, two named duplex NCs simultaneously |
| **JmxCreateNCTest** | Creates network connector via `BrokerViewMBean.addNetworkConnector()`; verifies JMX MBean registration |
| **JavaNetworkConnectorTest** | Runtime add/update/remove of DiscoveryNetworkConnector via JavaRuntimeConfigurationBroker; verifies included/excluded destinations are preserved, duplicate adds are idempotent, JMX MBean lifecycle |
| **JaasDualAuthenticationNetworkBridgeNioSslTest** | Configuration-only subclass for SSL transport variant; inherits JAAS dual auth network bridge tests from parent |
| **DestinationBridgeAccessorsTest** | Validates XBean XML configuration parsing for destination bridge accessors via `XBeanBrokerFactory.createBroker()` |

| Test | Summary |
|---|---|

Directly inspects `Destination.getConsumers()` |
| **MQTTNetworkOfBrokersFailoverTest** | Configures broker with `mqtt-virtual-topic-subscriptions` strategy plugin; uses `VirtualTopic` destination interceptor for MQTT topic-to-queue mapping. End-to-end MQTT durable subscription over a virtual topic network |

### Category 5: Tests About Message Groups

| Test | Summary |
|---|---|
| **MessageGroupLateArrivalsTest** | The only message group test in the candidate set. Sets `JMSXGroupID` on messages assigned to groups A, B, C. Starts 2 consumers first (claim groups A, B), then starts a 3rd consumer late. Sends group C messages. Validates: the late consumer exclusively receives group C messages, each consumer receives only its assigned group(s), group stickiness is maintained. Exercises consumer control code paths (group-to-consumer assignment in broker Queue dispatch) |

### Category 6: Tests About Message Interceptors

| Test | Summary |
|---|---|
| **MessageInterceptorTest** | Primary interceptor test. Registers `MessageInterceptor` via `MessageInterceptorRegistry` on broker. Tests: (a) normal delivery with no interceptor, (b) stack-overflow prevention when re-injecting in interceptor, (c) silent message drop, (d) re-routing from topic to queue by changing destination, (e) wildcard topic matching (`&gt;`) for interceptor registration. Exercises the `MessageInterceptorFilter` pipeline but does not go into broker region |
| **FailoverStateTrackingTest** | Uses `BrokerPluginSupport` to intercept `addConsumer`/`addProducer` calls. First rejects all (simulating auth failure), then after broker restart allows them. Validates failover transport state-tracking: failed operations are not retried. Exercises consumer/producer control interception |
| **BrokerQueueNetworkWithDisconnectTest** | Uses `BrokerPlugin` to inject sleep delays during connection removal (`removeConnection`) to simulate load/stress on connection cleanup |
| **JavaNetworkConnectorTest** | Uses `JavaRuntimeConfigurationBroker` which is a broker interceptor plugin; exercises the runtime configuration interceptor pipeline |
| **RedeliveryPluginTest** | Tests `RedeliveryPlugin` (which is a `BrokerPlugin` implementation). Validates that plugin installation fails when: no scheduler support, initialRedeliveryDelay too small, redeliveryDelay too small |

| **AMQ6477Test** | Tests reduceMemoryFootprint on dispatched messages for DURABLE queue subscriptions. Accesses PrefetchSubscription internals via reflection |
| **TwoBrokerDurableForwardStaticRestartTest** | Durable subscription forwarding across 3-broker chain. Pre-registers inbound durable subs on network bridges. Verifies message recovery after intermediate broker restart |
| **MQTTNetworkOfBrokersFailoverTest** | MQTT durable subscription (QoS 1, clean=false) failover across broker network. Validates no duplicate delivery after reconnection |

## Completed: Regression Test Prioritization for commit a37c111 (AMQ-9475)

### Output Files
- **JSON**: `ranked_testcases.json` (111 entries)
- **CSV**: `ranked_testcases.csv` (111 data rows)

### Change Analyzed
`AbstractRegion.processConsumerControl()` — prevents auto-creation of wildcard/pattern/composite destinations when processing ConsumerControl commands. The lookup method now accepts an `autoCreate` flag that gates destination auto-creation.

### Key Conclusion
**No regression failures predicted** (`predicted_regression=false` for all 111 candidates). The change is a targeted bug fix (AMQ-9475) that prevents unwanted auto-creation of wildcard destinations. The dedicated regression test (`AMQ9475Test`) is not in the candidate set. No candidate test sends ConsumerControl to a wildcard destination, which is the only scenario that could exercise the changed code path differently.
